# Transcrição: 65. Trait 64 - Great Salespeople believe their personal value exceeds the value of their product or service.

- **Fonte:** 65. Trait 64 - Great Salespeople believe their personal value exceeds the value of their product or service.
- **Duração:** 01:51
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:51]

Great salespeople believe their personal value, what they bring to the game exceeds the value of their product, their service, their company and their offering. Look, the great salespeople aren't confused. They don't try a little shortcut. So I gotta put a perfect suit on, perfect tie on, I gotta have the perfect shoes on, I gotta have the right coat, the bag. You know, that bag's gotta look perfect. Look, the greats are like, dude, I walk in any room, anytime dressed, anyway, with any bag, and I'm shutting something down. Why? Because my value, my value in this proposition exceeds the value of the product, the service, my company and our offering. Look, your value in the game, if you want to be great, your value, people would want to do business with you, not just the product or service you have. They're just like enamored by you, the idea of you, the greatness of you, the relentless follow-up of you. If people aren't asking you to go work for them or run their sales department, look, you're not even in the neighborhood of great. The greats are getting offers all the time because people see their value. Dude, who are you, man? Who are you, man? You know, that's what people, people need to be treating you like you're like some kind of superhuman, some Avenger. Are you one of the Avengers? Are you a Disney character? Are you out of a fantasy? Who are you? See, that's when your personal value exceeds the value of your product or service. That's when you're great. Look, be great. Be great as a dad, a parent. Be great as the teacher in your community, the salesperson. Be great as the fundraiser for your church. When your personal value exceeds the value of your product, your offering, your company. When your personal value is so big, people are like, I got to do business with this guy. It makes no sense. I don't have the money. I shouldn't do it. It's going to bank for others. I still got to do it. I got to do it. Personal value exceeds the value of your product.
